name: Baked New Potato Salad with Peanuts and Musta
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Small red new potatoes - scrubbed
Freshly ground black pepper - to taste
1 large Carrot - sliced on the diagonal 1/4-inch thick
2 medium Ribs celery - sliced on the diagonal 1/4-inch thick
2 tbsp Finely chopped cilantro - fresh, or parsley
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the peanuts in a small baking pan and toast in a preheated 350-degree F oven for 5 minutes, or until the skins start to crack. Cool slightly. Rub in a kitchen towel to remove the skins; chop coarsely.
2. Cut the potatoes into quarters and place in a baking pan. Toss with the olive oil, salt and several grindings of pepper. Bake in a preheated 375-degree F oven for 30 minutes or until tender. Stir occasionally.
3. Remove from the oven and toss with the vinegar. Let cool slightly.
4. Place the diced red onion in a small bowl and cover with ice-water. Let sit for 20 minutes to reduce the acidity. Drain and squeeze out the excess moisture with a paper towel. Bring a small pan of water to a boil, add the carrots and cook for 2 minutes. Drain and rinse with cold water to stop the cooking. Pat dry.
5. Combine the cooled potatoes, onion, carrots and celery in a large bowl. Stir together the mayonnaise, yogurt, cilantro, mustards, cumin and several grindings of black pepper. Combine with the vegetables and refrigerate. Stir in the peanuts just before serving.
